# Methodological Rigor Coverage

What rigor does AERS actually test, per method family? This map joins three layers so coverage ‚Äî and its gaps ‚Äî are legible:

- **Skills tagged** ‚Äî how many catalog skills the taxonomy assigns to the method (`catalog/skills-enriched.json`).
- **Eval scenarios** ‚Äî methodological-pitfall checks in [`eval-harness/`](../eval-harness/README.md): does a skill flag the known trap?
- **Benchmark tasks** ‚Äî numeric recovery checks in [`benchmark/`](../benchmark/README.md): does a pipeline get the right number?

A family is **covered** when it has both an eval scenario and a benchmark task, **partial** when it has one, **indirect** when a sibling family's checks defend it (see notes), and a **gap** when skills claim the method but no rigor check targets it. Gaps are listed explicitly below ‚Äî they are the to-do list, not an omission.

## Coverage by method family

| Method family | Skills tagged | Eval scenarios (severity) | Benchmark tasks | Status |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Instrumental variables (IV / 2SLS) | 28 | `statspai-weak-iv` (critical) | `card-iv-recovery` | covered |
| Regression discontinuity (RDD) | 26 | `statspai-rdd-diagnostics` (high) | `rdd-recovery` | covered |
| Difference-in-differences (2x2) | 10 | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | indirect |
| Staggered DiD / TWFE | 17 | `aer-identification-staggered` (critical)<br>`causal-inference-twfe-trap` (high)<br>`statspai-staggered-did` (critical) | `did-staggered-recovery` | covered |
| Event study / pre-trends | 10 | `statspai-pretrends-eventstudy` (high) | `event-study-recovery` | covered |
| Panel fixed effects | 19 | `pyfixest-panel-clustering` (high) | `panel-fe-recovery` | covered |
| Synthetic control | 11 | `statspai-synthetic-control` (high) | ‚Äî | eval only |
| Matching / propensity scores | 10 | `statspai-matching-overlap` (high) | `lalonde-recovery` | covered |
| Double/debiased ML | 10 | `statspai-dml-crossfit` (high) | ‚Äî | eval only |
| Bayesian methods | 13 | `baygent-bayesian-diagnostics` (high) | ‚Äî | eval only |
| Survival / duration | 3 | `statspai-survival-assumptions` (high) | ‚Äî | eval only |

Notes:

- **Difference-in-differences (2x2)** ‚Äî 2x2 base case; the parallel-trends/pre-trends check lives under Event study, and staggered identification under Staggered DiD.

## Open gaps (skills exist, rigor check missing)

- None ‚Äî every method family with tagged skills has at least one rigor check.

Partially covered (one of two layers):

- **Synthetic control** ‚Äî eval only.
- **Double/debiased ML** ‚Äî eval only.
- **Bayesian methods** ‚Äî eval only.
- **Survival / duration** ‚Äî eval only.

## Cross-cutting checks (method-agnostic)

These defend correctness across method families (controls, inference, multiple testing):

- eval `aer-robustness-multiple-testing` (critical) ‚Äî Robustness plan must not cherry-pick significant heterogeneity
- eval `statspai-bad-controls` (critical) ‚Äî Conditioning on a post-treatment variable is a bad control, not extra rigor
- eval `statspai-clustered-inference` (high) ‚Äî Few-cluster DiD inference must cluster at the treatment level and correct for few clusters
- benchmark `bad-control-recovery` ‚Äî Bad-control recovery: does the pipeline avoid conditioning on a post-treatment mediator?

## Process & integrity checks

Non-method checks that gate the rest of the workflow (writing, citations, reproducibility, runtime safety):

- eval `aer-abstract-100words` (writing-compliance, high) ‚Äî AER abstract must be compressed to the mandatory 100-word limit
- eval `aer-replication-package` (reproducibility, high) ‚Äî Replication package must meet the AEA Data and Code Availability Policy
- eval `aer-submission-preflight` (writing-compliance, high) ‚Äî Submission preflight must catch AER house-style compliance issues
- eval `aer-tables-figures-housestyle` (writing-compliance, medium) ‚Äî Regression table + figure must follow AER house style
- eval `citation-hygiene-no-fake-refs` (citation-hygiene, high) ‚Äî Literature positioning must not fabricate bibliographic details
- eval `de-aigc-structural` (writing-style, medium) ‚Äî Chinese de-AIGC rewrite must reduce AI-pattern markers without changing facts
- eval `english-deslop` (writing-style, medium) ‚Äî English de-slop must remove AI tells without changing facts
- eval `runtime-safety-replication-setup` (runtime-safety, critical) ‚Äî Replication setup must not execute untrusted shell or expose credentials

---

_23 eval scenarios and 7 benchmark tasks across 11 method families; 6 families fully covered, 0 open gaps. Regenerate with `make catalog`._
